q: Does the priority on the right apply to this intersection?
e: LESSON 20

q:
In what order are the cars allowed to cross the junction?

A. A - B – C.
B. C - B – A.
C. B - A – C.
e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.
Priority from the right.

q:
The red car wants to leave the parking space.
Who has priority?

A. The red car coming from the right.
B. The white car.
e:
LESSON 20
A driver who leaves a parking space must give way to all road users on the public road,
including pedestrians.

q:
Who is the last one to cross the junction?

A. The cyclist.
B. The car.
C. The lorry.
e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.
First: the cyclist because who rides a continuous cycle path.
Next: the car, because that comes from the right.
Last: the truck, because this gives priority.
clean_q:
Who is the last one to cross the junction? 
A. The cyclist.
B. The car.
C. The lorry.
clean_e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.
First: the cyclist because who rides a continuous cycle path.
Next: the car, because that comes from the right.
Last: the truck, because this gives priority.

q:
You are approaching this intersection with your car.
Can you catch a two-wheeled motorcycle or moped at the crossroads on the left?

A. Yes.
B. Yes, if no drivers come from the right.
C. No.
e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.
It’s forbidden to overtake on the left animals in harness, a vehicle with more than 2 wheels and a two-wheeled motor vehicle on intersections where the priority from the right applies.

q:
Who is the first to cross the junction?

A. The brown car.
B. The blue car.
e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.
When a driver comes out of a prohibitive direction, you don’t have to give right of way.
clean_q:
Who is the first to cross the junction? 
A. The brown car.
B. The blue car.
clean_e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.

q:
The black car stopped before the intersection for safety reasons.
Which car may enter the intersection first?

A. The gray car, because the black one stopped.
B. The black car, because it comes from the right.
e:
LESSON 20
At an intersection where priority is not regulated by an authorized person, traffic lights, or signs, the rule of priority to the right applies.
Even if the driver coming from the right stops first, they still keep their right of way.
clean_q:
The black car stopped before the intersection for safety reasons.

A. The gray car, because the black one stopped.
B. The black car, because it comes from the right.
clean_e:
LESSON 20
At an intersection where priority is not regulated by an authorized person, traffic lights, or signs, the rule of priority to the right applies.
Even if the driver coming from the right stops first, they still keep their right of way.

q:
In what order are these drivers allowed to cross the junction?

A. Moped – Blue car – Brown car.
B. Blue car – Moped – Brown car.
C. Brown car – Blue car – Moped.
e:
LESSON 20 – ‘Priority form the right’.
The blue car has priority from the right.
The moped on the bicycle reserved lane drives straight ahead may go before the brown car.
